summary: Backend-only testing for Beyond The Blades foundation API. 17/18 pytest tests passed. All validation, CRUD, Stripe checkout creation, MongoDB no-_id-leak, and donation package enumeration work correctly. Single failure on /api/donations/status/{session_id} is due to an upstream Stripe error ('No such checkout.session') with the shared test key sk_test_emergent — not a server bug.

minor.issue: After creating a checkout session successfully, immediate status retrieval fails with Stripe API error 'No such checkout.session: cs_test_...'. Server maps this to HTTP 502. Transaction record with payment_status='initiated' IS created correctly in MongoDB (verified indirectly). Likely caused by emergentintegrations StripeCheckout library or the shared sk_test_emergent key routing creation and retrieval to different Stripe accounts. Real user flow (Stripe redirects user → webhook fires) is not affected because webhook path updates the record without calling get_checkout_status.

action_items: Investigate why StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status() returns 'No such checkout.session' immediately after create_checkout_session() succeeds with sk_test_emergent. Consider: (a) adding a small retry/delay in /donations/status/{session_id}, (b) falling back to the stored DB record if Stripe returns not-found, or (c) verify the emergentintegrations library uses the same API key for create and retrieve. Front-end polling on /donate/success page will currently show 502 until Stripe webhook finalizes the record.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses @app.on_event('shutdown') which is deprecated in FastAPI; prefer lifespan context manager.

webhook endpoint calls _get_stripe which relies on request.base_url — in production with proxy, base_url may not match the public URL unless trust_proxy/forwarded-headers middleware is enabled. Stripe webhook_url must match what was registered; for now it's constructed per-request which is fine but fragile.
In /donations/checkout, when only custom_amount is provided, DONATION_PACKAGES is bypassed (correct), but metadata['package_id'] becomes 'custom' — consistent. Good.
EmailStr serialization: emitted as str in responses OK (pydantic v2 handles).
No rate limiting on public POST endpoints (volunteers, contact, activity-signups) — spam risk. Consider adding throttling.
CORS set to '*' with allow_credentials=True — browsers will reject; not urgent since app uses Bearer-less public API.
Single-file server.py is small (~320 lines) so splitting is not required yet.
test_credentials: N/A (no auth required for tested endpoints). Stripe: sk_test_emergent in backend/.env.
seed_data_creation: Created test records with prefix 'TEST_' in volunteers, activity_signups, contact_messages, and payment_transactions collections. Not cleaned up (no delete endpoints exist).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use /app/backend/tests/test_backend_api.py as regression suite. The only failure is TestCheckout::test_status_champion — caused by upstream Stripe 'No such checkout.session' error. If the main agent adds retry/fallback logic in /api/donations/status/{session_id}, this test should start passing. All validation (422), business-rule (400), and MongoDB _id exclusion assertions pass. No frontend testing performed — request explicitly scoped backend-only.
rca of the issue: RCA for status-check failure: (1) POST /api/donations/checkout creates Stripe session via emergentintegrations.StripeCheckout.create_checkout_session → returns valid session_id starting cs_test_. (2) Record inserted into payment_transactions with payment_status='initiated'. (3) Immediate GET /api/donations/status/{session_id} calls StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status(session_id) which hits Stripe API and receives 'No such checkout.session'. (4) Exception is caught and re-raised as HTTP 502. Reproduction: POST /api/donations/checkout with {package_id:'champion', origin_url:<any>}; immediately GET /api/donations/status/<returned session_id>. Expected 200, got 502. Mitigation options: (a) on Stripe not-found, return the stored DB record with payment_status='initiated' instead of 502; (b) retry with 1-2s backoff before failing; (c) verify emergentintegrations uses the same sk_test key for create and retrieve paths.
